items.curatedReasonEn: Preliminary findings on developmental outcomes in 4-year-old children born during England's first COVID-19 lockdown suggest the impact of environmental factors on early child development. This reminds you to consider the potential effects of broader social environments, such as pandemics, when assessing child development.
items.limitation: 该研究为初步发现，样本量和具体数据未在摘要中详细说明，可能存在地域性限制。
items.limitationEn: This study presents preliminary findings; sample size and specific data are not detailed in the abstract, and there may be regional limitations.

items.title: 115 Early childhood development in the next 1000 days: an analysis through the lens of nurturing and sensitive care
items.summary: This abstract presents an analysis of early childhood development in the next 1000 days through the lens of nurturing and sensitive care, emphasizing the importance of supportive environments.
items.curatedReason: 在“未来 1000 天”内，通过养育性和敏感性照护视角分析的儿童早期发展，强调了早期干预的重要性。你可以将此作为你向家长宣教和建议的依据。
items.titleZh: “未来 1000 天”内儿童早期发展：通过养育性和敏感性照护视角进行的分析
items.summaryZh: 这份摘要通过养育性和敏感性照护的视角，分析了“未来 1000 天”内儿童早期发展，强调了支持性环境的重要性。
items.curatedReasonEn: Early childhood development analyzed through the lens of nurturing and sensitive care in the “next 1000 days” emphasizes the importance of early intervention. You can use this as a basis for educating and advising parents.
items.limitation: 这是一项会议摘要，信息有限，无法评估具体研究方法和结果。
items.limitationEn: This is a conference abstract with limited information, making it impossible to evaluate specific research methods and results.

items.title: Biomechanical patterns associated with body mass index in percutaneous transforaminal endoscopic decompression models of elderly lumbar spinal stenosis with severe disc degeneration and osteoporosis: a finite element study
items.summary: A finite element study investigated BMI-related biomechanical patterns in elderly lumbar spinal stenosis patients with severe disc degeneration and osteoporosis following percutaneous transforaminal endoscopic decompression (PTED), focusing on postoperative kinematics and load redistribution.
items.curatedReason: 这项有限元研究探讨了老年腰椎管狭窄症患者经皮椎间孔内窥镜减压术（PTED）后，BMI 对生物力学模式的影响。目前该研究属于工程力学建模范畴，不能直接指导临床康复方案，但可作为了解手术对脊柱生物力学影响的参考。
items.titleZh: BMI 对经皮椎间孔内窥镜减压术治疗重度椎间盘退变和骨质疏松老年腰椎管狭窄症生物力学模式的影响：一项有限元研究
items.summaryZh: 一项有限元研究调查了 BMI 对经皮椎间孔内窥镜减压术 (PTED) 治疗重度椎间盘退变和骨质疏松老年腰椎管狭窄症患者生物力学模式的影响，重点关注术后运动学和载荷再分配。
items.curatedReasonEn: This finite element study explores the impact of BMI on biomechanical patterns after percutaneous transforaminal endoscopic decompression (PTED) in elderly patients with lumbar spinal stenosis. Currently, this study falls within the scope of engineering mechanics modeling and cannot directly guide clinical rehabilitation protocols, but it can serve as a reference for understanding the biomechanical effects of surgery on the spine.
items.limitation: 这是一项有限元研究，属于计算机模拟，结果的临床转化性和现实应用性有待进一步验证。
items.limitationEn: This is a finite element study, which is a computer simulation; the clinical translatability and real-world applicability of its results require further validation.

items.title: Acupressure Improves Quality of Life During Radiotherapy for Head and Neck Cancer: Study
items.summary: Researchers found that traditional and auricular acupressure improved physical, psychological, social, environmental, and overall quality of life in head and neck cancer patients undergoing radiotherapy, suggesting it may be a safe, low-risk supportive therapy.
items.curatedReason: 传统和耳穴按压法改善了头颈癌放疗患者的生活质量。尽管证据仍在累积中，你可以在临床上将按压作为安全、低风险的辅助疗法，帮助患者缓解治疗期间的不适。
items.titleZh: 研究：按压疗法改善头颈癌放疗期间的生活质量
items.summaryZh: 研究人员发现，传统和耳穴按压法改善了头颈癌放疗患者的生理、心理、社会、环境和整体生活质量，表明它可能是一种安全、低风险的辅助疗法。
items.curatedReasonEn: Traditional and auricular acupressure improved quality of life for head and neck cancer radiotherapy patients. While evidence is still accumulating, you can clinically use acupressure as a safe, low-risk adjunctive therapy to help patients alleviate discomfort during treatment.
items.limitation: 这是一篇新闻报道，基于一项研究的结论，但未提供研究的具体方法、样本量和详细数据，无法评估证据等级。
items.limitationEn: This is a news report based on a study's conclusion, but it does not provide specific methods, sample size, or detailed data from the study, making it impossible to assess the level of evidence.

items.title: Measurement Characteristics and Clinical Utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test Among People with Stroke
items.summary: This study focuses on the measurement characteristics and clinical utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) in individuals with stroke.
items.curatedReason: 卒中患者的 6 分钟步行测试（6MWT）仍是功能评估的重要工具，但你知道它在不同亚组里的测量特性和敏感性差异吗？了解这些细节能帮你更好地解读结果，并根据患者具体情况调整你的康复策略。
items.titleZh: 卒中患者 6 分钟步行测试的测量特性和临床应用价值
items.summaryZh: 本研究探讨了卒中患者 6 分钟步行测试（6MWT）的测量特性和临床应用价值。
items.curatedReasonEn: The 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) remains a vital tool for assessing function in stroke patients. However, do you know the differences in its measurement characteristics and sensitivity across various subgroups? Understanding these details will help you better interpret results and tailor your rehabilitation strategies to the patient's specific condition.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: [Perspectives] The art of fidelity: clinical documentation and ambient AI
items.summary: A perspective piece highlights the current limitations of ambient AI in clinical documentation, noting its tendency to miss crucial patient narratives like caregiving burden and medication adherence issues, even while accurately recording medical facts.
items.curatedReason: 人工智能在医疗记录中的应用正在改变工作流程，但你也应警惕其在抓取患者核心问题时的局限性，尤其是那些未被直接言明的社会心理因素。
items.titleZh: 人工智能病历：忠实度与临床洞察的平衡
items.summaryZh: 一篇评论文章指出，环境人工智能在临床记录方面存在局限性，它可能忽略患者重要的叙述，如护理负担和药物依从性问题，即使其医学记录准确无误。
items.curatedReasonEn: AI's application in medical documentation is transforming workflows, but you should be wary of its limitations in capturing core patient issues, especially unspoken psychosocial factors.

items.title: Modified Ghost System combining action, observation, and vibration stimulation for recovery after distal radius fracture surgery: A single-arm clinical feasibility study protocol
items.summary: A single-arm feasibility study protocol outlines a modified Ghost System for distal radius fracture rehabilitation, replacing VR headsets with an iPad and wristband vibrator to improve clinical implementation and patient access. The study will enroll 10 adults post-palmar locking plate fixation.
items.curatedReason: Ghost System 的改良版，用 iPad 和腕带振动器替代笨重的 VR 头显，大大提升了临床应用的便捷性。关注这项可行性研究，看其能否为腕部骨折术后的康复训练提供新的思路。
items.titleZh: 改良 Ghost 系统结合行动观察与振动刺激，用于桡骨远端骨折术后康复：一项单臂临床可行性研究方案
items.summaryZh: 一项单臂可行性研究方案描述了一种改良的 Ghost 系统，用于桡骨远端骨折康复。该系统用 iPad 和腕带振动器替代 VR 头显，旨在提高临床应用和患者的可及性。研究将招募 10 名掌侧锁定钢板固定术后的成年患者。
items.curatedReasonEn: The modified Ghost System, replacing bulky VR headsets with an iPad and wristband vibrator, significantly enhances clinical usability. Follow this feasibility study to see if it offers new approaches for post-distal radius fracture rehabilitation.
items.limitation: 这是一项单中心、单臂的试点可行性研究，样本量小（10 名），且没有对照组，结果的外推性有限。
items.limitationEn: This is a single-center, single-arm pilot feasibility study with a small sample size (10 participants) and no control group, limiting generalizability.
